If the rate is around 2.42%... that doesn't make sense as a standard rate.

Hmm, let me look at this differently. Maybe the Gold Rewards Card earns 2.5% on most categories and a different rate on some categories.

Nordstrom: $320 at Shopping, earned 775 points.

775/320 = 2.421875. Hmm that's not clean.

223/89.34 = 2.496... ≈ 2.5%. So 2.5% on Target.

And Nordstrom: $320 - Shopping - 775 points.
775/320 = 2.42... 

Actually let me do the math differently. 2.5% of $320 = $8.00 = 800 points. But we have 775. 

775 points = $7.75. $7.75/$320 = 2.421875%.

That's an odd number. Unless... maybe there's some specific category calculation issue?

Actually, let me re-examine. What if the Gold Rewards Card has a base rate and some categories earn different rates? I've seen reference to the Silver Rewards Card having 4% on Travel/Software, Diamond Elite having 5% on everything...
